title: Board Creation from Templates via Automation - Board Ownership Assignment
excerpt:
I have found a post that has over time detailed this issue/request - however it doesnt appear to have resulted into anything substancial despite the 21 upvotes.

posts.author: luke_c (Luke Crampton)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have found a post that has over time detailed this issue/request - however it doesnt appear to have resulted into anything substancial despite the 21 upvotes.

Auto assign board owner based on people column - Feature requests - monday Community Forum

I have an issue which I believe is shared within the community with regards to allowing more control on template boards being created from automations - relating to the ownership of a board. At the moment there is functionality to choose board owners and subscribers for a board creation via automation, however a person is required for selection rather than something dynamic and this causes problems.

We have a set process in which users submit a form, and dependant on the options selected as part of the form submission the relevant template will be generated in the workspace. Any user in the workspace could log this so its impossible to set persons as pre-defined owners in the automations. The user who has submitted the form should have ownership of the board in this use case. This information is captured as part of the form subission, however we’re unable to use the dynamic field as far as I can tell.

I write this hoping that users have faced simiar experiences and have aomse form of fix, or that monday pick this up to progress. I’m happy to also be shown there is a way to meet this use-case, as ive found it a re-ooccuring issue over some time.

Thanks.

Luke

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @luke_c You’re not missing anything. Today, when an automation creates a board, the owner has to be a fixed person. You can’t dynamically set the board owner from a People field or a form response yet.

So if anyone in the workspace can submit the form and should become the board owner, there’s no native way to make that happen during board creation right now.

What people usually do instead:

Check the “Created by” field after the board is made and update the owner from there.
Have an admin or ops role get notified and quickly switch the board owner based on the form info.

The best long term fix is to keep supporting the feature request so product sees how common this need is.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Monday Wizard
